reviews.review_description: I've wanted this for a while kinda like stranger things vr but longer and more stories it has all of the movies and TV shows and maybe a sandbox mode or a hunter mode where you are a stormtrooper hunting bounty hunters and Jedi to turn them in. Just a thought but please make this

reviews.review_title: Star Wars perfection but with a caveat
reviews.review_description:
Vader immortal episode three is an incredibly fun VR games to play and the best one in the entire series. The main story is very enjoyable￼ and the new iteration of the Lightsaber dojo has a perfect blend of both action packed fun and a challenge that will make you want to curse your head off

This game has only three negatives I can think of first off the ￼ main story is quite short ￼ being able to finish it in less than an afternoon 

Secondly, this game doesn’t give you much freedom. You are confined to certain paths, and there are some sections where you’re not able to move at all me personally I don’t mind this too much but I know it will be for some players 

and this last issue is more of a bug, but in the Vader duel, sometimes ￼￼his saber just kind of phases through yours, and the blocking doesn’t work right I’m not sure if this is a personal mistake. I’m making or a bug, but it really takes out some of the immersion for me ￼￼

reviews.review_title: A Short but Brilliant VR Star Wars Exper
reviews.review_description:
I played through all three episodes of the Vader Immortal trilogy, and honestly, I really enjoyed it. Yes, each part is pretty short on its own, but experiencing them together as one complete story feels very satisfying. I took my time exploring the environments and ended up spending about two hours across all three episodes. The locations are gorgeous, and the atmosphere pulls you right in.
Just a heads-up: this isn’t an action-heavy game. It’s more of an interactive cinematic experience, but it’s executed really well, with some genuinely epic moments. The lightsaber mechanics are a real highlight - training, swinging the blade, and deflecting blaster bolts feel incredibly satisfying and immersive in VR, even though the combat stays simple and accessible.
I’m not a hardcore Star Wars fan these days, but I grew up watching the films and loved them. Getting to step back into that universe in VR after all these years was a real treat. Standing face-to-face with Vader actually gave me chills.
At full price, the length might feel a bit brief for some, but on sale, the complete trilogy is an absolute steal. If you’re looking for a polished, atmospheric VR adventure that lets you live out a few iconic Star Wars moments, this is absolutely worth picking up.

reviews.review_title: Amazing but missing a lot of stuff
reviews.review_description:
Don't get me wrong I love this game but it's missing a lot of updates that will boost up the downloads. 

1# multiplayer and battle Royale would make the 10x funner

2# maps that people can play and create maps so other people could play them

3# free roam imagine obliterating some stormtroopers while not even play the game

So that's all my suggestion and to the guy that made this master piece, pls add this it would make it 1000x more fun.

Honestly, if you are a big fan of Star Wars and are just looking for a short fun game to play, you might like this, but for the casual fan that is just looking for an action game related to Star Wars, this is a letdown. The game is very very short and uneventful, it took me about 45 minutes to complete it and the only memorable part was the end fight with Darth Vader. The lightsaber was one of the bright spots as I thought it was pretty cool, but it doesn't make up for the bad movement mechanics and short story. Overall for 10 bucks it was somewhat worth it but if you are expecting and exciting action game this just isnt for you.
I’m a Star Wars fan so that’s why I decided to download it but after I found out, you can use the force and you can block bullets and throw your light saber that was that made it my favorite game ￼
Vader was underwhelming as heck and all the three games are way too short and underwhelming, you dont even get one shotted by a lightsaber.
Beat vr game I’ve ever played but only had a few hours worth of content if you ever come back to this system consider making a clone wars era game
It’s fun but lacks really anything to be a game. Like literally never played the other two and beat the game in a solid 10 minutes. Also characters lwk look like sht
I love the game but I just hate that there’s no episode 4 please nabey you can add a free play like tales from the galaxy’s edge
I've played all three of them and this was probably the best you feel like you'r actually on the planet fighting highly recommended by an all of them

Me personally, I think they should make more games.         I finished all three games SO quick.              They really need more games and a multiplayer mode so you can light saber duel with your friends.   But overall the games are great
Awesome

This trilogy is truly remarkable. Despite its brevity, it remains an excellent experience. I genuinely hope that the developers behind this project will create more lightsaber-centric games or ones set between the original trilogy and the prequels.

** MY OPINION (complete trilogy): A HUGE FAN FAVORITE! ** EPIC, GRANDIOSE, MASTERFUL… AND ABOVE ALL, INCREDIBLY IMMERSIVE! Visually stunning, Vader Immortal impresses with its lighting effects, iconic environments, legendary sound effects (the lightsaber), and a soundtrack worthy of the films. It's incredibly easy to pick up and play, fully customizable, and motion sickness-free: a perfect entry point into VR, even for beginners. 🔴 Episode I: brilliant setup, the first encounter with Darth Vader is impressive! 🔴 Episode II: shorter but packed with revelations and iconic scenes! 🔴 Episode III: intense and memorable finale, a truly apotheosis! SHORT BUT INTENSE Each episode is relatively short (45–60 minutes if you take your time), but the DOJOS change everything. They offer tremendous replayability! Up to 3 hours per dojo, with progressively increasing difficulty, and combined use of the lightsaber AND the Force in the final episode. 😈 MEET DARTH VADER IN VR! Seeing him, fighting alongside him, then facing him… it's an emotional rollercoaster and an experience every Star Wars fan simply must have. This trilogy alone justifies buying a Quest for any fan of the saga. A childhood dream come true. THE PROS - THE CONS Total Star Wars immersion. Excellent VR comfort. Accessible to beginners. Ultra-fun and long-lasting dojos. Darth Vader… simply amazing! But the main adventure is rather short. We always want more 😉 🎯 CONCLUSION: AN ABSOLUTE HIT! Vader Immortal – The Trilogy is an unmissable VR experience, to be played in one sitting. A narrative gem, spectacular and emotional, designed for both fans and newcomers to VR.

reviews.review_title: About the 3 games and my opinion
reviews.review_description: What I really find cool is that the end of episode 1 is the beginning of episode 2 and the ending of episode 2 is the beginning of episode 3. Overall everything about the games are just so AMAZING like the characters the graphics the music the animations all of it is just so GOOD. What has been crossing my mind is that what happened to the guy (aka the player) and the droid go? Wich planet or galaxy did they go to? And what was there adventer like? And what treasure did they find together? Allthough there is a glitch were you get stuck on the loading screen but i can easily get past that.  Other than that I love all 3 games and i wish i could see more games that are created by the same creators that made these 3 awsome games.
Best game ever the story is so good and I hade a really fun time finishing the whole series

It's just not that worth it. The trailer makes it look fun but it's just not that good . The story takes like thirty minutes and like half of it is just talking. The lightsaber dojo is ok, but it doesn't add much. Overall a 3/10.

All three games fit together perfectly with a little overlap everything looks good and works well  would be cool if we could get another game continuing their story
Loved the game, just wish it wasn’t so short!
One of the best games I’ve ever played but 1 problem. As far as the challenge thing, we NEED to be able to use the sabers in story mode we’ve unlocked in the wave thing, it’s a MUST

With the version 83 OS release Meta has fixed whatever they broke in their previous OS  release and Vader Immortal is working on Quest 3 now.

While I'm glad I can play this VR classic again, it's disconcerting that Meta and / or game developers can take away games you've purchased at any time with buggy software updates.

Note that the developer responses to bug reports are worthless. If you send an email to the address given you get a reply back saying that they don't support the Quest version and to contact Meta.

So no one maintains Vader Immortal's code.

It's abandonware at the mercy of Meta's ever changing buggy OS.

This is my first review for Quest 3. These 3 games are what I want from a Star Wars VR game! The story is good and run, but the training mode is the best part. Now if there were a Jedi adventure game like that but where you can walk around... I'll play it all day long! Definitely buy these games!

This game is great but me and my cousins want to fight each other with lightsabers but we cant do that if there is no multiplayer. And I think a fourth episode will be amazing with the adventures of you character.

reviews.review_description: i highly recommend this game and i really wanna 4th one heck maybe in a 5th one

Honestly waste of money. I got the game expecting it to be good, the story mode feels like the opening mission to any game…. But that’s the whole game! Thought I was missing something at the credits screen like it was the beginning scene of a Star Wars movie where the story gets described to set the stage, but no those were ending credits. Even went back to the “chapters” to make sure I didn’t miss something and, drum roll please, they considered walking between scenes as full chapters. Walk ten steps put some crystals in chapter walk ten more steps perform three hand waves chapter walk three more steps on to ship travel for 3 minutes while fighting 10-20 enemies chapter fight through 2 corridors of enemies 2 more chapters walk ten steps chapter 1 semi decent boss fight end game

The entire game felt like a "stand here and listen. Now walk 10 feet and stand here and listen some more." There was no point where my lightsaber made contact with anyone other than Vader in the final battle and he died with 2 strikes. When I saw the credits I was so surprised because every scene felt like a training course for the real game.
....to meta for updating the oculus to allow these games to work again. Love playing this.

I purchased all 3 episode and was impressed...however, I felt like they were to short and lacking combat.

They could have made this epic!  I feel that you barely use the skills that you use in training.

Could you imagine if they had Battlefront or the Force Unleashed for the Quest 3!

The dojo challenge, is close to perfection, dual saber only, is so much fun and good exercise for those of us who would normally be sitting on a couch with a pad in hand. 

If each set of levels had a main character (Jedi/Sith/Assassin) from "Star wars" movies (jump from spot to spot ) close in using a melee weapon , far off using blaster or lightning bolt that you need to reflect/block. With some interesting defence and strike pointers (like arcade hints highlighting strike points) and an exciting slash opening into slow motion  where you get a moment to go mad (like killer is dead and Metal Gear Rising and have to slice through the enemy from one end to another as fast as you can with the points system registering the speed and length of each ark/slice carving down the life of the boss/"Star wars" main character)?

This would then be the perfect "Star wars" VR arcade game classic. I would pay £30-£60 if it kept the gameplay and dynamic fun of the 3rd dojo as it is. 

(I would also add difficulty settings and a different landscape for each boss). 

The shooting/blaster tracking, feels a bit off also. However!! I purchased the 3 parts of the main game on the PS4 pro, and a hard copy to hoard as a classic. 

It was inevitable that I'm having it here on the "quest 3" as well (to try 360 mode).

5/5!!!

Works well on the Quest 2. At least 30 minutes of gameplay in the main story.
The battles are great (hard to aim with the guns).
The dojo is very good. It requires skill and experience to advance in the levels.

30 minute story includes 25 minutes of dialogue with 5 minutes of "battles" this seemed like such a fun game and the reviews seemed great but I just feel scammed and scalped because of just how much you could do with this concept like driving tie fighters of THROWING THE LIGHTSABER YOU START WITH. But this was just nothing but dialogue I had to grab a chair because of how tired my legs got from standing while waiting for the ability to move on to of having to hear the most annoying NPC talk on and on

When I go into the dojo, the audio cuts out with this horrendous short noise signaling it as soon as the enemies start appearing. It’s genuinely unplayable this way and I can’t play my favorite part of the game without the audio because of it. Please fix this. Oh yeah, I’m going on Quest 2 just so you know. This only happens in Episode 3.

EDIT: okay i managed to play without the thing happening but I don’t know what caused it or if it will happen again so I can’t quite give it a 5. Good game though.

reviews.review_title: The Disney trilogy is better than this..
reviews.review_description:
Come on guys, the VR system is the best system to make a Star wars game. This franchise had so much potential but it's all wasted. The hitbox for the playable character is all over the place and the story is SOOOO short, like I spent 30 on the story and almost all of that is just dialogue, and whenever the player comes across a fighting section it's so short and boring. The only fun part of the game is like the final levels of lightsaber dojo and even during that the hitboxes are acting up so you can't even play that. You people wasted this game and y'all could've made so much money. Star wars on VR has to be the greatest idea ever but not this.

Update:This game is just disappointing, please I beg of you to not buy this game, at least not until they actually make an good update on this
It’s fun but I wish you could do more
It was very cool to fight darth vader in the end and use blasters at stormtroopers

reviews.review_title: This game has its pros and cons
reviews.review_description: This is a good game I like playing the dojo but the setting don't work I have controller drift in my right controller and it tends to spin me around a lot when I try to play dojo and I've pressed off on the turning and it doesn't turn it off

reviews.review_title: Epic battle between one-man Jedi vs. Imp
reviews.review_description:
Even though this is the very first series of Star Wars VR games, the game was professionally developed as it harbours great quality in many aspects. The overall quality is very high, and it never disappoints Star Wars fans.

The graphics are highly detailed. The sound and music are epic, as all Star Wars media should be. The atmosphere, the motion, the special effects, the environment, the scene transitions, the feeling—everything looks great. They truly draw out all the essences of Star Wars and put them together in this VR experience. This is what a Star Wars VR game is meant to be.

Although the story length in each episode is quite short, it is very enjoyable. In this Episode 3, the story progresses to the final showdown against the Imperials, where the protagonist must utilise both lightsabre and Force expertise learned and practised from previous episodes—along with additional armaments—to overcome the Imperial threat. The story of this episode is considered the best experience, as it offers the role of a powerful Jedi to solo intense battles against the Empire.

The Lightsabre Dojo mode in this Episode 3 is enhanced with new Force abilities and usable armaments. The best thing is the addition of an iconic enemy, the Stormtrooper, to join the fight.

I wish it was longer, but good game overall
They have light saber Training I like that you can throw your light saber in it ￼￼￼ When you throw it at ￼storm trooper It stabs them ￼￼
￼ Definitely continues story And I hope they come out with a fourth to finish it 
￼￼ Sad you can’t throw your light saber in the actual game
￼ I’m excited for part four ￼

So as the second once that was completed this was an immediate purchase. Once played as obvious as it sounds the first two were a build up training you to use the skills learned in unison to complete the game. Far better gameplay than the second and also the first, much more engaging than the second however still short. 
The overall experience of the 3 combined at £24 is worth it even though they are short. The experience can be extended with the training sims however as my review mentioned in the first episode the tasks could be more challenging to extend the experience. 
To sum up if you love Star Wars and are not too fussed about the cost then buy all 3 as you will love the experience. If you want more bang for buck then perhaps look for longer games for your money.
This is a must play for all meta quest owners! Great game! I wish there was more content and combat!

you can’t move whatsoever when using your saber, i actually had to stop playing because the block mechanic only works ever 1 in like 3 blocks, it was fun for five minutes then you just feel like you’re chained to the floor, like the dojo my main issue is when the blaster droids spawn you have to reach so far forward to actually hit them that i have to use the force for 90% of my gameplay just to kill things, had so much potential but ultimately is just a star wars story where legs don’t work
Make it so we don’t have to spend over £20 to play this game please

The game overall was fun, and the visuals were creative, but the episode was way too short. There were also no consequences getting hit injured as you didn't have a health bar. I guess it's called immortal for a reason. Overall the game was super fun and it felt like I was inside of Star wars but it was certainly not worth $10. If it was 10 times the length it would have been, but here's your warning. Don't buy Vader immortal. I know this is really redundant, but I'm just really trying to get my message across. Do not waste $10 on buying Vader immortal.
If you looking for an action filled game with fighting and exploring this is the game for you. I love star wars and I love adventure games this is both combined.
